title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Evaluation Brief: Rollback Experiment
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt rollback experiment increment in a production prompt portfolio and release gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.
Monitor outcome, refusal, tool, latency, cost, and drift signals without logging sensitive content unnecessarily.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Rollback Experiment
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Rollback Experiment contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.

content.text: Use a changed theory case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Rollback Experiment condition decided your prediction.

options.text: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Prompt changes are too small to require release controls. This does not resolve predict rollback experiment.

For predict rollback experiment, apply Rollback Experiment without its stated preconditions, omit the a production prompt portfolio and release gate trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Rollback Experiment for a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

steps.title: Bound Rollback Experiment
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Rollback Experiment application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.

content.text: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check the Rollback Experiment preconditions, operate on a production prompt portfolio and release gate in an inspectable order, then verify its result and boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Rollback Experiment, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt changes are too small to require release controls. This fails the Rollback Experiment contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Prompt changes are too small to require release controls. This does not resolve bound rollback experiment.

Use Rollback Experiment after naming its inputs, invariant or ownership condition, observable evidence, and failure boundary.

steps.title: Repair the Rollback Experiment defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Rollback Experiment failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Prompt changes are too small to require release controls.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.

options.text: For repair the rollback experiment defect, apply Rollback Experiment without its stated preconditions, omit the a production prompt portfolio and release gate trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Rollback Experiment for a production prompt portfolio and release gate.
Reconstruct the conditions for Rollback Experiment, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the Rollback Experiment artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. This does not resolve repair the rollback experiment defect.

steps.title: Build Rollback Experiment evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-rollback-experiment-1v4cko" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rollback Experiment into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-rollback-experiment-1v4cko
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Rollback Experiment structural check is only one gate. a production prompt portfolio and release gate must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Sequence Rollback Experiment evidence

steps.title: Defend the Rollback Experiment model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Rollback Experiment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed theory case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.
